title: Project Telos Nineteenth-Wave Finite Edge/Operator Replay Claims

claims.text: The nineteenth-wave package adds a bounded Lean finite edge/operator replay rung while keeping smooth periodic integration by parts NOT_REPLAYED and the parent Millennium problem UNVERIFIABLE.
claims.falsification: The package is missing, lacks the finite edge/operator replay artifact, or claims the smooth PDE theorem or Navier-Stokes parent problem is proved.

claims.text: The nineteenth-wave theorem is stored in a separate Lean file and preserves the fourteenth-, fifteenth-, sixteenth-, and seventeenth-wave source hashes.
claims.falsification: The nineteenth theorem mutates a prior Lean source file, or the recorded prior source hashes no longer match.

claims.text: FiniteEdgeOperatorPreflight.lean compiles with Lean exit code 0, emits no Lean stdout/stderr, and replays the explicit finite edge/operator declarations and theorem.
claims.falsification: The Lean file is missing, the replay receipt is missing, the command does not exit 0, warnings or stderr appear, or the package claims the finite edge/operator theorem proves the smooth PDE theorem.

claims.text: The nineteenth-wave source refresh retains 20 arXiv metadata rows, 19 unique IDs, verifies both Gather stores as MATCH, and demotes the rows to SOURCE_LEAD_ONLY.
claims.falsification: The source demotion gate is missing, counts differ, store verification does not match, or metadata is promoted as paper truth.

claims.text: The nineteenth-wave routing summary records Forum routing, source-checkout Index success, installed Index wrapper failure, and missing Telos PATH wrapper without promoting wrapper health.
claims.falsification: The route summary is missing, omits the wrapper failure, omits the source-checkout Index MATCH, or claims installed wrapper health.

claims.text: The website copy and official-copy scaffold distinguish FINITE_EDGE_OPERATOR_SBP_MATCH from NOT_REPLAYED smooth theorem claims.
claims.falsification: Any updated publication surface collapses the finite edge/operator replay into a smooth periodic integration-by-parts proof or omits the NOT_REPLAYED boundary.

claims.text: The nineteenth-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries for Navier-Stokes, smooth periodic integration by parts, source truth, exhaustive coverage, native BuildLang relation receipts, wrapper health, and accepted-publication claims.
claims.falsification: The content queue is missing or omits those boundaries.
